messages.content: yi numbers follow base ten system like chinese one is cip two is nip three is suo four is lir five is nga six is fur seven is shi eight is hxi nine is gu ten is ci these are similar across yi dialects

yi language follows sov word order subject object verb unlike chinese which uses svo subject verb object for example i eat rice in chinese is wo chi fan but in yi it would be wo fan eat the verb always comes at the end

yi verbs do not conjugate for person or number tense is marked by particles before or after the verb past tense uses particles like da present has no marker future uses particles like guo this is simpler than chinese which uses le or guo
water in yi is ꒉ yi this is one of the most basic and ancient words in the yi language water is essential in yi culture for agriculture and spiritual practices

in yi language adjectives come after the noun they modify this is the opposite of chinese for example red flower in chinese is hong hua adjective first but in yi it would be hua hong noun first this is an important grammatical difference

yi language has tones most dialects have 3 to 4 tones high mid low and sometimes rising tones distinguish word meaning like chinese mandarin has 4 tones tone changes can change the entire meaning of a word
fire in yi is ꃅ mu fire holds sacred significance in yi culture the torch festival is the most important yi celebration honoring fire as a purifying and life giving force
person or human in yi is ꊿ co this is the root word for many related concepts like people community and humanity yi philosophy centers on human flourishing and harmony with nature
heart or mind in yi is ꑭ ni in yi culture the heart is the seat of both emotion and wisdom it appears in many idioms and philosophical expressions about understanding and compassion
sun in yi is ꊿꆹ mup lie which literally means sky fire this shows how yi creates compound words from basic elements sky and fire combine to form sun
sky or heaven in yi is ꃅ mu this word also appears in compound words like sun sky fire and thunder the sky is considered the domain of the supreme deity in yi cosmology

yi negation uses negative particles placed before the verb similar to chinese bu the most common negative particle in yi is a or mot negation of past tense uses different particles than present or future tense

yi sentence structure has these key features sov word order adjectives after nouns postpositions instead of prepositions classifiers for counting question particles at sentence end negative particles before verbs these features make yi typologically similar to tibetan burman and japanese

yi nouns can be modified by adjectives which come after the noun this is opposite to chinese where adjectives come before nouns yi also uses classifiers like chinese but the classifier comes between the number and noun

yi language uses classifiers similar to chinese and southeast asian languages classifiers come between numbers and nouns different types of objects take different classifiers for example flat objects long objects and round objects each have specific classifiers

yi questions are formed by adding question particles at the end of the sentence similar to chinese ma particle the most common question particles in yi are a and ma yes no questions use these particles while content questions use question words like what who where
moon in yi is ꀊꁌ a bu which relates to the ancestral and cyclical nature of the moon in yi culture the moon represents the mother principle and cycles of time
mountain in yi is ꃅ bo yi people traditionally live in mountainous regions of sichuan yunnan and guizhou mountains are central to yi identity and spiritual beliefs
